mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses the threshold, or out-of-sample Sharpe ratio < 0.1.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand and construction sector activity, which translates into higher corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in > 60% of triggered cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days during periods of stable inflation.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative when INDPRO accelerates during stable inflation, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 3.0% annually.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 245.0, persistent inflationary pressures force central banks to maintain restrictive policy, increasing equity risk premiums and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in > 50% of months where CPIAUCSL remains above 245.0, or the threshold effect vanishes in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a strengthening labor market that supports wage growth and consumer demand, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict positive returns in > 55% of observations, or the interaction effect is negative in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product reflects expanding economic output and corporate profit fundamentals, which directly supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical relationship with GDPC1 growth rates, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 across all rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the index of industrial production exceeding 0.5 percent signals a contraction in manufacturing activity and supply chain bottlenecks, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of instances where INDPRO drops below the threshold, or the signal shows zero predictive accuracy in out-of-sample tests.
